---
title: "Model backtesting and calibration"
subtitle: "Evaluating prediction quality, signal value, and hyperparameter tuning"
format: html
---

# Configuration

```{julia}
#| echo: true
#| output: false
using Velogames, DataFrames, Statistics, MarkdownTables

# Which years to backtest (VG historical pages available for 2023–2025)
backtest_years = [2023, 2024, 2025]

# Simulation settings
n_sims = 1000
history_years = 5

# Risk aversion (0 = risk-neutral baseline, >0 penalises high-variance riders)
risk_aversion = 0.0

# Whether to run the (slow) tuning sections
run_tuning = true

# Cache (longer TTL for historical data that won't change)
bt_cache = CacheConfig(joinpath(homedir(), ".velogames_cache"), 168)

# Helpers
md_table(df) = print(sprint(show, MIME("text/markdown"), markdown_table(df)))

cat_label = Dict(1 => "Cat 1 (monuments)", 2 => "Cat 2 (WT classics)", 3 => "Cat 3 (semi-classics)")
```

# Race catalogue

```{julia}
#| output: asis
races = build_race_catalogue(backtest_years; history_years=history_years)

cat_counts = combine(
    groupby(
        DataFrame(year=[r.year for r in races], category=[r.category for r in races]),
        :year
    ),
    nrow => :n_races
)

print("""
Backtesting across **$(length(races)) races** from $(join(string.(backtest_years), ", ")).

""")
md_table(cat_counts)
print("\n")
```

```{julia}
#| output: false
# Pre-fetch all race data once — reused by baseline, ablation, and tuning
race_data = suppress_output() do
    prefetch_all_races(races; cache_config=bt_cache)
end
```

## Data availability

```{julia}
#| output: asis
# Summarise what signals are available per race
n_total = length(races)
n_fetched = length(race_data)
n_with_odds = count(d -> d.odds_df !== nothing, values(race_data))
n_with_oracle = count(d -> d.oracle_df !== nothing, values(race_data))
n_with_history = count(d -> d.race_history_df !== nothing, values(race_data))

print("""
| Signal | Races with data | Coverage |
|--------|-----------------|----------|
| PCS results (ground truth) | $n_fetched / $n_total | $(round(100 * n_fetched / n_total, digits=0))% |
| PCS race history | $n_with_history / $n_fetched | $(round(100 * n_with_history / max(n_fetched, 1), digits=0))% |
| Archived Betfair odds | $n_with_odds / $n_fetched | $(round(100 * n_with_odds / max(n_fetched, 1), digits=0))% |
| Archived Cycling Oracle | $n_with_oracle / $n_fetched | $(round(100 * n_with_oracle / max(n_fetched, 1), digits=0))% |

""")

if n_with_odds > 0 || n_with_oracle > 0
    print("Races with archived odds/oracle data:\n\n")
    archive_rows = []
    for (race, data) in sort(collect(race_data), by=p -> (p.first.year, p.first.name))
        has_odds = data.odds_df !== nothing
        has_oracle = data.oracle_df !== nothing
        if has_odds || has_oracle
            push!(archive_rows, (
                race=race.name,
                year=race.year,
                odds=has_odds ? "$(nrow(data.odds_df)) riders" : "—",
                oracle=has_oracle ? "$(nrow(data.oracle_df)) riders" : "—",
            ))
        end
    end
    if !isempty(archive_rows)
        md_table(DataFrame(archive_rows))
        print("\n")
    end
else
    print("""
::: {.callout-warning}
No archived odds or oracle data found. The `baseline+odds` and `baseline+oracle` ablation sets will behave identically to `baseline`. Run `solve_oneday` with `betfair_market_id` or `oracle_url` for upcoming races to start building the archive.
:::

""")
end
```

# Baseline evaluation

Running the full prediction pipeline on all historical races and comparing to actual PCS finishing positions.

```{julia}
#| output: asis
baseline_signals = [:pcs, :vg_season, :race_history, :vg_history, :form, :trajectory]

results = suppress_output() do
    backtest_season(races;
        race_data=race_data,
        signals=baseline_signals,
        n_sims=n_sims,
        risk_aversion=risk_aversion,
        store_rider_details=true,
    )
end

summary_df = summarise_backtest(results)

print("""
## Overall metrics

""")
# Show aggregate rows
agg = filter(:race => r -> startswith(r, "—"), summary_df)
md_table(agg[:, [:race, :n_riders, :spearman_rho, :top5_overlap, :top10_overlap, :mean_abs_rank_error, :points_captured_ratio]])
print("\n")
```

```{julia}
#| output: asis
# Spearman ρ distribution
valid_rho = filter(!isnan, [r.spearman_rho for r in results])
if !isempty(valid_rho)
    q25 = round(quantile(valid_rho, 0.25), digits=3)
    q75 = round(quantile(valid_rho, 0.75), digits=3)
    best = round(maximum(valid_rho), digits=3)
    worst = round(minimum(valid_rho), digits=3)

    print("""
## Spearman \$\\rho\$ distribution

The Spearman rank correlation measures how well predicted strength ranks match actual finishing positions. A value of 1.0 is perfect prediction; 0.0 is random.

| Statistic | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Best | $best |
| 75th percentile | $q75 |
| Median | $(round(median(valid_rho), digits=3)) |
| 25th percentile | $q25 |
| Worst | $worst |
| Races evaluated | $(length(valid_rho)) |

""")
end
```

## VG team points

```{julia}
#| output: asis
# Filter to races with VG team data
vg_results = filter(r -> !isnan(r.points_captured_ratio), results)

if !isempty(vg_results)
    print("""
### By year

| Year | Races | Mean PCR | Mean predicted pts | Mean optimal pts |
|------|-------|----------|--------------------|------------------|
""")
    for year in sort(unique(r.race.year for r in vg_results))
        yr = filter(r -> r.race.year == year, vg_results)
        pcr = round(mean(r.points_captured_ratio for r in yr), digits=3)
        pred = round(mean(r.predicted_team_vg_points for r in yr), digits=1)
        opt = round(mean(r.optimal_team_vg_points for r in yr), digits=1)
        print("| $year | $(length(yr)) | $pcr | $pred | $opt |\n")
    end
    print("\n")

    print("""
### By category

| Category | Races | Mean PCR | Mean predicted pts | Mean optimal pts |
|----------|-------|----------|--------------------|------------------|
""")
    for cat in sort(unique(r.race.category for r in vg_results))
        cr = filter(r -> r.race.category == cat, vg_results)
        pcr = round(mean(r.points_captured_ratio for r in cr), digits=3)
        pred = round(mean(r.predicted_team_vg_points for r in cr), digits=1)
        opt = round(mean(r.optimal_team_vg_points for r in cr), digits=1)
        label = get(cat_label, cat, "Cat $cat")
        print("| $label | $(length(cr)) | $pcr | $pred | $opt |\n")
    end
    print("\n")
else
    print("No races with VG team data available.\n\n")
end
```

# Calibration diagnostics

If the model's uncertainty estimates are well-calibrated, the standardised prediction errors (z-scores) should be approximately standard normal: mean near 0, standard deviation near 1, with roughly 68% of riders within one sigma and 95% within two sigma.

```{julia}
#| output: asis
all_z = vcat([r.calibration_z_scores for r in results]...)

if !isempty(all_z)
    z_mean = round(mean(all_z), digits=3)
    z_std = round(std(all_z), digits=3)
    cov1 = round(count(z -> abs(z) <= 1.0, all_z) / length(all_z), digits=3)
    cov2 = round(count(z -> abs(z) <= 2.0, all_z) / length(all_z), digits=3)

    print("""
## Aggregate calibration

| Statistic | Observed | Expected |
|-----------|----------|----------|
| Mean | $z_mean | 0.0 |
| Std | $z_std | 1.0 |
| 1σ coverage | $cov1 | 0.683 |
| 2σ coverage | $cov2 | 0.954 |
| Total rider-observations | $(length(all_z)) | — |

""")
end
```

```{julia}
#| output: asis
if !isempty(all_z)
    print("## Calibration by category\n\n")
    for cat in sort(unique(r.race.category for r in results))
        cat_z = vcat([r.calibration_z_scores for r in results if r.race.category == cat]...)
        if length(cat_z) < 10
            continue
        end
        cz_mean = round(mean(cat_z), digits=3)
        cz_std = round(std(cat_z), digits=3)
        cz_cov1 = round(count(z -> abs(z) <= 1.0, cat_z) / length(cat_z), digits=3)

        print("""
**$(get(cat_label, cat, "Cat $cat"))**: mean=$cz_mean, std=$cz_std, 1σ coverage=$cz_cov1 (n=$(length(cat_z)))

""")
    end
end
```

```{julia}
#| output: asis
if !isempty(all_z)
    print("## Calibration by year\n\n")
    for year in sort(unique(r.race.year for r in results))
        year_z = vcat([r.calibration_z_scores for r in results if r.race.year == year]...)
        if length(year_z) < 10
            continue
        end
        yz_mean = round(mean(year_z), digits=3)
        yz_std = round(std(year_z), digits=3)
        yz_cov1 = round(count(z -> abs(z) <= 1.0, year_z) / length(year_z), digits=3)

        print("""
**$year**: mean=$yz_mean, std=$yz_std, 1σ coverage=$yz_cov1 (n=$(length(year_z)))

""")
    end
end
```

## Q-Q table

```{julia}
#| output: asis
if !isempty(all_z)
    # Hardcoded standard normal quantiles (avoids Distributions.jl dependency)
    quantile_levels = [0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 0.9, 0.95, 0.99]
    expected_q = [-2.326, -1.645, -1.282, -0.674, 0.0, 0.674, 1.282, 1.645, 2.326]
    observed_q = [round(quantile(all_z, q), digits=3) for q in quantile_levels]

    print("""
| Quantile | Expected N(0,1) | Observed |
|----------|-----------------|----------|
""")
    for (q, e, o) in zip(quantile_levels, expected_q, observed_q)
        print("| $q | $e | $o |\n")
    end
    print("""

Systematic deviations indicate miscalibrated uncertainty. If observed quantiles are compressed relative to expected, the model is overconfident (uncertainties too narrow). If stretched, it is underconfident.

""")
end
```

## Calibration by strength tier

Splitting riders into strength tiers reveals whether calibration problems are concentrated among favourites, mid-pack riders, or outsiders.

```{julia}
#| output: asis
all_strengths = vcat([r.calibration_strengths for r in results]...)

if !isempty(all_z) && !isempty(all_strengths) && length(all_z) == length(all_strengths)
    # Split into quartiles by predicted strength
    q25_s = quantile(all_strengths, 0.25)
    q75_s = quantile(all_strengths, 0.75)

    tiers = [
        ("Bottom 25% (outsiders)", all_strengths .<= q25_s),
        ("Middle 50%", (all_strengths .> q25_s) .& (all_strengths .< q75_s)),
        ("Top 25% (favourites)", all_strengths .>= q75_s),
    ]

    print("""
| Tier | Mean z | Std z | 1σ coverage | n |
|------|--------|-------|-------------|---|
""")
    for (label, mask) in tiers
        tier_z = all_z[mask]
        if length(tier_z) < 10
            continue
        end
        tz_mean = round(mean(tier_z), digits=3)
        tz_std = round(std(tier_z), digits=3)
        tz_cov1 = round(count(z -> abs(z) <= 1.0, tier_z) / length(tier_z), digits=3)
        print("| $label | $tz_mean | $tz_std | $tz_cov1 | $(length(tier_z)) |\n")
    end
    print("\n")
end
```

# Signal analysis

## Signal contribution

Mean |shift| per signal shows how much each data source moves the predicted strength away from the uninformative prior, averaged across riders that have that signal.

```{julia}
#| output: asis
shift_keys = [:shift_pcs, :shift_vg, :shift_form, :shift_trajectory, :shift_history, :shift_vg_history, :shift_oracle, :shift_odds]
shift_labels = Dict(
    :shift_pcs => "PCS specialty",
    :shift_vg => "VG season points",
    :shift_form => "PCS form",
    :shift_trajectory => "Trajectory",
    :shift_history => "PCS race history",
    :shift_vg_history => "VG race history",
    :shift_oracle => "Cycling Oracle",
    :shift_odds => "Betfair odds",
)

# Aggregate across all races
agg_shifts = Dict{Symbol,Vector{Float64}}()
for r in results
    for (k, v) in r.mean_signal_shifts
        push!(get!(agg_shifts, k, Float64[]), v)
    end
end

if !isempty(agg_shifts)
    print("""
| Signal | Mean |shift| | Std | Races with signal |
|--------|-----------|-----|-------------------|
""")
    for k in shift_keys
        if !haskey(agg_shifts, k)
            continue
        end
        vals = agg_shifts[k]
        nonzero = filter(!=(0.0), vals)
        if isempty(nonzero)
            continue
        end
        label = get(shift_labels, k, string(k))
        print("| $label | $(round(mean(abs, nonzero), digits=3)) | $(round(std(nonzero), digits=3)) | $(length(nonzero)) |\n")
    end
    print("\n")
end
```

```{julia}
#| output: asis
if !isempty(agg_shifts)
    print("""
::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
### Signal shifts by category

""")
    for cat in sort(unique(r.race.category for r in results))
        cat_results = filter(r -> r.race.category == cat, results)
        cat_shifts = Dict{Symbol,Vector{Float64}}()
        for r in cat_results
            for (k, v) in r.mean_signal_shifts
                push!(get!(cat_shifts, k, Float64[]), v)
            end
        end

        print("**$(get(cat_label, cat, "Cat $cat"))**\n\n")
        print("| Signal | Mean shift | Races |\n|--------|-----------|-------|\n")
        for k in shift_keys
            if !haskey(cat_shifts, k)
                continue
            end
            vals = cat_shifts[k]
            nonzero = filter(!=(0.0), vals)
            if isempty(nonzero)
                continue
            end
            label = get(shift_labels, k, string(k))
            print("| $label | $(round(mean(nonzero), digits=3)) | $(length(nonzero)) |\n")
        end
        print("\n")
    end
    print(":::\n\n")
end
```

## Signal ablation study

Testing which data sources improve predictions by removing one signal at a time from the baseline. Each row shows the mean metric across all races for that signal configuration.

```{julia}
#| output: asis
ablation_df = suppress_output() do
    ablation_study(races;
        race_data=race_data,
        n_sims=500,
    )
end

if nrow(ablation_df) > 0
    # Extract aggregate rows only
    agg_ablation = filter(:race => r -> r == "— MEAN —", ablation_df)

    if nrow(agg_ablation) > 0
        display_abl = sort(agg_ablation, :spearman_rho, rev=true)
        md_table(display_abl[:, [:signal_set, :spearman_rho, :top5_overlap, :top10_overlap, :mean_abs_rank_error]])
        print("\n")
    end
end
```

```{julia}
#| output: asis
if nrow(ablation_df) > 0
    agg_ablation = filter(:race => r -> r == "— MEAN —", ablation_df)
    if nrow(agg_ablation) > 0
        best_set = first(sort(agg_ablation, :spearman_rho, rev=true)).signal_set
        worst_set = last(sort(agg_ablation, :spearman_rho, rev=true)).signal_set

        print("""
::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
### Interpreting the ablation

**Best configuration:** `$best_set` | **Worst:** `$worst_set`

Each `no_X` row shows what happens when signal X is removed from the baseline. If removing a signal hurts performance, that signal adds value. Compare against `no_signals` (uninformative prior) and `pcs_only` as floor references.

Note: VG season points use cumulative-to-date values (not end-of-season totals) to maintain temporal integrity. The remaining limitation is that this relies on VG deadline dates being parseable for each race. Odds and oracle signals are only available for races where archived data exists.
:::

""")
    end
end
```

# Ranking detail

## Results by year

```{julia}
#| output: asis
for year in sort(unique(r.race.year for r in results))
    year_results = filter(r -> r.race.year == year, results)
    if isempty(year_results)
        continue
    end
    year_rhos = filter(!isnan, [r.spearman_rho for r in year_results])
    if isempty(year_rhos)
        continue
    end

    print("""
### $year

Races: $(length(year_results)) | Mean Spearman ρ: $(round(mean(year_rhos), digits=3)) | Median: $(round(median(year_rhos), digits=3))

""")
end
```

## Results by category

```{julia}
#| output: asis
for cat in sort(unique(r.race.category for r in results))
    cat_results = filter(r -> r.race.category == cat, results)
    if isempty(cat_results)
        continue
    end
    cat_rhos = filter(!isnan, [r.spearman_rho for r in cat_results])
    if isempty(cat_rhos)
        continue
    end

    print("""
### $(get(cat_label, cat, "Cat $cat"))

Races: $(length(cat_results)) | Mean Spearman ρ: $(round(mean(cat_rhos), digits=3)) | Median: $(round(median(cat_rhos), digits=3))

""")
end
```

```{julia}
#| output: asis
print("""
::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Per-race results

""")

race_rows = filter(:race => r -> !startswith(r, "—"), summary_df)
display_df = sort(race_rows, :spearman_rho, rev=true)
md_table(display_df[:, [:race, :year, :category, :n_riders, :spearman_rho, :top10_overlap, :mean_abs_rank_error]])
print("""

:::

""")
```

## Worst predictions

The races with the lowest Spearman ρ, with the biggest individual prediction misses for each.

```{julia}
#| output: asis
# Find 5 worst races by Spearman ρ
worst_results = sort(filter(r -> r.rider_details !== nothing, results), by=r -> r.spearman_rho)
n_worst = min(5, length(worst_results))

if n_worst > 0
    for r in worst_results[1:n_worst]
        detail = sort(r.rider_details, :rank_error, rev=true)
        top_misses = first(detail, min(5, nrow(detail)))

        print("""
### $(r.race.name) $(r.race.year) (ρ = $(round(r.spearman_rho, digits=3)))

| Rider | Predicted rank | Actual rank | Rank error |
|-------|----------------|-------------|------------|
""")
        for row in eachrow(top_misses)
            print("| $(row.riderkey) | $(row.predicted_rank) | $(row.actual_rank) | $(row.rank_error) |\n")
        end
        print("\n")
    end
else
    print("No rider-level detail available.\n\n")
end
```

# Hyperparameter sensitivity

```{julia}
#| echo: true
#| output: asis

if run_tuning
    # Tune against the baseline signal set with calibration-aware objective and CV
    best_config, tuning_log = suppress_output() do
        tune_hyperparameters(races;
            race_data=race_data,
            objective=:calibrated_rho,
            n_iter=50,
            signals=baseline_signals,
            n_sims=n_sims,
            cv=true,
        )
    end

    print("""
## Best configuration

""")

    default_params = Dict(
        :pcs_variance => DEFAULT_BAYESIAN_CONFIG.pcs_variance,
        :vg_variance => DEFAULT_BAYESIAN_CONFIG.vg_variance,
        :form_variance => DEFAULT_BAYESIAN_CONFIG.form_variance,
        :trajectory_variance => DEFAULT_BAYESIAN_CONFIG.trajectory_variance,
        :hist_base_variance => DEFAULT_BAYESIAN_CONFIG.hist_base_variance,
        :hist_decay_rate => DEFAULT_BAYESIAN_CONFIG.hist_decay_rate,
        :vg_hist_base_variance => DEFAULT_BAYESIAN_CONFIG.vg_hist_base_variance,
        :vg_hist_decay_rate => DEFAULT_BAYESIAN_CONFIG.vg_hist_decay_rate,
        :signal_correlation => DEFAULT_BAYESIAN_CONFIG.signal_correlation,
        :vg_season_penalty => DEFAULT_BAYESIAN_CONFIG.vg_season_penalty,
    )

    print("""
| Parameter | Default | Tuned | Change |
|-----------|---------|-------|--------|
| `pcs_variance` | $(default_params[:pcs_variance]) | $(round(best_config.pcs_variance, digits=2)) | $(round(best_config.pcs_variance - default_params[:pcs_variance], digits=2)) |
| `vg_variance` | $(default_params[:vg_variance]) | $(round(best_config.vg_variance, digits=2)) | $(round(best_config.vg_variance - default_params[:vg_variance], digits=2)) |
| `form_variance` | $(default_params[:form_variance]) | $(round(best_config.form_variance, digits=2)) | $(round(best_config.form_variance - default_params[:form_variance], digits=2)) |
| `trajectory_variance` | $(default_params[:trajectory_variance]) | $(round(best_config.trajectory_variance, digits=2)) | $(round(best_config.trajectory_variance - default_params[:trajectory_variance], digits=2)) |
| `hist_base_variance` | $(default_params[:hist_base_variance]) | $(round(best_config.hist_base_variance, digits=2)) | $(round(best_config.hist_base_variance - default_params[:hist_base_variance], digits=2)) |
| `hist_decay_rate` | $(default_params[:hist_decay_rate]) | $(round(best_config.hist_decay_rate, digits=2)) | $(round(best_config.hist_decay_rate - default_params[:hist_decay_rate], digits=2)) |
| `vg_hist_base_variance` | $(default_params[:vg_hist_base_variance]) | $(round(best_config.vg_hist_base_variance, digits=2)) | $(round(best_config.vg_hist_base_variance - default_params[:vg_hist_base_variance], digits=2)) |
| `vg_hist_decay_rate` | $(default_params[:vg_hist_decay_rate]) | $(round(best_config.vg_hist_decay_rate, digits=2)) | $(round(best_config.vg_hist_decay_rate - default_params[:vg_hist_decay_rate], digits=2)) |
| `signal_correlation` | $(default_params[:signal_correlation]) | $(round(best_config.signal_correlation, digits=2)) | $(round(best_config.signal_correlation - default_params[:signal_correlation], digits=2)) |
| `vg_season_penalty` | $(default_params[:vg_season_penalty]) | $(round(best_config.vg_season_penalty, digits=2)) | $(round(best_config.vg_season_penalty - default_params[:vg_season_penalty], digits=2)) |

""")
else
    print("""
Hyperparameter tuning is disabled (`run_tuning = false`). Set `run_tuning = true` in the configuration cell to enable it.

""")
end
```

```{julia}
#| output: asis
if run_tuning && @isdefined(tuning_log) && nrow(tuning_log) > 0
    print("""
## Search landscape

Top 10 candidates by calibrated score (Spearman \$\\rho\$ with calibration penalty, leave-one-year-out CV):

""")
    top10 = first(sort(tuning_log, :mean_score, rev=true), min(10, nrow(tuning_log)))
    display_cols = [:candidate, :mean_score, :n_races, :pcs_variance, :vg_variance, :form_variance, :trajectory_variance, :hist_base_variance, :hist_decay_rate, :signal_correlation]
    avail_cols = intersect(display_cols, Symbol.(names(tuning_log)))
    round_numeric_columns!(top10; digits=3)
    md_table(top10[:, avail_cols])
    print("\n")
end
```

```{julia}
#| output: asis
if run_tuning && @isdefined(tuning_log) && nrow(tuning_log) > 1
    scores = filter(!isnan, tuning_log.mean_score)
    if !isempty(scores)
        default_score = first(filter(:candidate => ==("default"), tuning_log)).mean_score
        print("""
::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Tuning summary

Searched $(nrow(tuning_log)) configurations using leave-one-year-out cross-validation. Score range: $(round(minimum(scores), digits=4)) to $(round(maximum(scores), digits=4)).

The default configuration scored $(round(default_score, digits=4)). The objective combines Spearman ρ with a penalty for miscalibrated uncertainty (z-score std deviating from 1.0).
:::

""")
    end
end
```

# Risk aversion tuning

The optimiser can penalise high-variance riders using `risk_adjusted_vg = E[pts] / (1 + γ * CV_down)`. This section searches over γ values to find the level that maximises the points captured ratio in backtesting.

```{julia}
#| echo: true
#| output: asis

if run_tuning
    best_gamma, gamma_log = suppress_output() do
        tune_risk_aversion(races;
            race_data=race_data,
            signals=baseline_signals,
            n_sims=n_sims,
        )
    end

    print("""
## Risk aversion grid search

**Best γ:** $(best_gamma)

""")

    round_numeric_columns!(gamma_log; digits=4)
    md_table(gamma_log)
    print("\n")
else
    print("""
Risk aversion tuning is disabled (`run_tuning = false`). Set `run_tuning = true` in the configuration cell to enable it.

""")
end
```

# Domestique discount tuning

The domestique discount penalises non-leaders within each team proportionally to their strength gap from the team leader, reducing their simulated finishing positions. This section searches over discount values to find the level that maximises points captured.

```{julia}
#| echo: true
#| output: asis

if run_tuning
    best_discount, discount_log = suppress_output() do
        tune_domestique_discount(races;
            race_data=race_data,
            signals=baseline_signals,
            n_sims=n_sims,
            risk_aversion=risk_aversion,
        )
    end

    print("""
## Domestique discount grid search

**Best discount:** $(best_discount)

""")

    round_numeric_columns!(discount_log; digits=4)
    md_table(discount_log)
    print("\n")
else
    print("""
Domestique discount tuning is disabled (`run_tuning = false`). Set `run_tuning = true` in the configuration cell to enable it.

""")
end
```
